title: Mutations and pending UI

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific React: Component-Based Browser Applications draft for Mutations and pending UI; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.claim: A mutation models pending, success, validation failure, conflict, retry, and cache reconciliation while preventing accidental duplicates.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A mutation models pending, success, validation failure, conflict, retry, and cache reconciliation while preventing accidental duplicates.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A save button disables duplicate submission and merges the server-returned version after success. Your goal is to implement and test a remote mutation with disabled-state and error-recovery semantics. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Cleanup and race prevention; javascript network fetch; Client fetch lifecycle; Validation and error recovery. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: A mutation models pending, success, validation failure, conflict, retry, and cache reconciliation while preventing accidental duplicates. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Optimistic updates need rollback, retries need idempotency, and server truth can reject client assumptions. This draft therefore rejects: Optimistic UI means the server result can be ignored once local state changes.

includes: Coordinate form mutation, pending feedback, success reconciliation and recoverable failure without duplicate submission.
Implement and test a remote mutation with disabled-state and error-recovery semantics.
excludes: Optimistic updates need rollback, retries need idempotency, and server truth can reject client assumptions.

outcomes: Implement and test a remote mutation with disabled-state and error-recovery semantics.

examples.title: Mutations and pending UI: concrete case
examples.situation: A save button disables duplicate submission and merges the server-returned version after success.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A save button disables duplicate submission and merges the server-returned version after success.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Reconcile mutations with authoritative server results and roll back or explain failed optimistic changes.
examples.result: The result follows because Reconcile mutations with authoritative server results and roll back or explain failed optimistic changes.

Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: Optimistic UI means the server result can be ignored once local state changes.
